evidence_quotes.quote: protecting customer rights when it comes to video games.

Currently there exist no customer protections that prevent video game companies from halting a customer's access to a video game they have already bought, at any time and with practically no warning no less.
This destruction of an already bought product has already happened to hundreds of video games, and hundreds more are at risk right now.
The movement is pushing for video game developers to implement end-of-life plans to games in the future that would otherwise be at risk.
The Digital Fairness Act could also bring to table consumer protections that support a customer's right to continued function and access of a video game that has already been sold to them.

paragraphs.text: Hello! As a citizen of the EU I would like to bring to attention the currently ongoing movement called Stop Killing Games. It is very much in spirit of the Digital Fairness Act, and pertains to protecting customer rights when it comes to video games. In short: Currently there exist no customer protections that prevent video game companies from halting a customer's access to a video game they have already bought, at any time and with practically no warning no less. This destruction of an already bought product has already happened to hundreds of video games, and hundreds more are at risk right now. The movement is pushing for video game developers to implement end-of-life plans to games in the future that would otherwise be at risk. This can be as simple as making an online game have offline functions, or allow players to host the necessary servers. The Digital Fairness Act could also bring to table consumer protections that support a customer's right to continued function and access of a video game that has already been sold to them. As it stands, the Stop Killing Games movement has already gathered over 1,400,000 signatures on a European Citizens' Initiative titled "Stop Destroying Videogames"! In addition, multiple members of the european parliament have voiced their support of the movement, including the Vice President of the European Parliament Nicolae Stefanuta! The people of the European Union are unanimous in their wish to protect their consumer rights, and we are voicing our hope for that to finally include video games! Thank you, for your continued work towards protecting our rights as customers, and for reading through my request!
